LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT/CROSS DEFENDANT MAO FENG'S MOTION TO DETERMINE GOOD FAITH OF SETTLEMENT [CCP 887.6(A)(1)] WITH PLAINTIFF JIA WEI FENG IS GRANTED. GIVEN A REASONABLE ESTIMATE OF SPECIAL DAMAGE, THE RELATIVE CULPABILITY OF THE SETTLING DEFENDANT AND THE ABSENCE OF ANY EVIDENCE OF COLLISSION OR THAT THIS IS NOT AN ARMS LENGTH AGREEMENT, THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT IS IN THE BALL PARK OF LIKELY OUTCOMES DISCOUNTED BY THE UNCERTAINTY OF TRIAL AND IS IN GOOD FAITH. OTHER PARTIES CLAIMS AGAINST THE SETTLING DEFENDANT FOR IMPLIED OR EQUITABLE INDEMINTY INCLUDING CAUSE OF ACTION 5 AND 6 IN QUEST'S CROSS-COMPLAINT ARE DISMISSED PURSUANT TO C C P SECTION 877.6 (C), OTHER CROSS-CLAIMS ARE NOT DISMISSED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

APPEARANCES- ATTY. NANCY ENG OF THE LAW FIRM OF DARLING & RISBROUGH, LLP, TEL. NO. (714) 384-4250 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCE ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT/CROSS DEFENDANT MAO FENG'S Motion To Determine Good Faith Of Settlement [Ccp 887.6(A)(1)] WITH PLAINTIFF JIA WEI FENG IS GRANTED. GIVEN A REASONABLE ESTIMATE OF SPECIAL DAMAGE, THE RELATIVE CULPABILITY OF THE SETTLING DEFENDANT AND THE ABSENCE OF ANY EVIDENCE OF COLLISSION OR THAT THIS IS NOT AN ARMS LENGTH AGREEMENT, THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT IS IN THE BALL PARK OF LIKELY OUTCOMES DISCOUNTED BY THE UNCERTAINTY OF TRIAL AND IS IN GOOD FAITH. OTHER PARTIES CLAIMS AGAINST THE SETTLING DEFENDANT FOR IMPLIED OR EQUITABLE INDEMINTY INCLUDING CAUSE OF ACTION 5 AND 6 IN QUEST'S CROSS-COMPLAINT ARE DISMISSED PURSUANT TO C C P SECTION 877.6 (C), OTHER CROSS-CLAIMS ARE NOT DISMISSED. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol A. Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/PM

Off Calendar Aug-07-2008 Text Ruling

Mark S. Julius, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Forte International
Shun c. Chen, Esq., for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Jia Wei Feng

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant Forte International, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Further Responses To Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, is granted.
